title: Pan Seared Broccolini
subtitle: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish

steps.text: Peel and dice the sweet potato(es). Add diced sweet potato to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and return cooked sweet potato to the saucepan, off heat. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils, and add to a separate saucepan with 1 cup (2 cups) water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender, 20 to 24 minutes.

Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim, halve, and deseed the Fresno chile(s), and mince one half (1 Fresno chile). Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Trim 1 inch off the broccolini stems.

Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook until lightly browned, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced Fresno chile, minced ginger, golden raisins, toasted coconut, brown mustard seeds, and turmeric. Stir and cook until aromatic, 1 to 3 minutes. Add lemon juice and transfer the coconut turmeric relish to a medium bowl.

Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium-high heat. Add 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, ¼ cup (½ cup) water, and a pinch of salt. Add broccolini, cover, and cook until water evaporates and broccolini is tender and crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

Add butter to the cooked sweet potatoes and mash with a fork. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide the mashed sweet potatoes and beluga lentils between large plates. Top with pan seared broccolini and coconut turmeric relish. Enjoy!

tips: TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.
TIP: Save the remaining ginger for your own use.
TIPS: Add more Fresno chile if you prefer more heat, and save any remaining chile for your own use. We will use this skillet again in the next step.
